purpose.cmd/scip-search: CLI entrypoint wiring commands, flags, output modes, and query handlers
purpose.internal/cli: Runtime command routing, argument validation, index loading, status codes, and build identity
purpose.internal/query/discovery: Symbol and package discovery queries, including name matching and JSON/one-line payloads
purpose.internal/query/graph: Static SCIP graph and impact queries for definitions, incoming references, outgoing dependencies, relationships, test hints, and compact agent-oriented output
purpose.internal/query/implementations: Implementation lookup from SCIP relationship edges with structured, one-line, and location-only output
purpose.internal/query/oneline: Shared source-location formatting helpers for grep-style query output
purpose.internal/query/references: Reference lookup for exact SCIP symbols with structured, one-line, and location-only output
purpose.internal/runtime: Shared runtime contracts for loaded indexes, raw output, JSON success, diagnostics, and exit statuses
purpose.internal/scipindex: SCIP protobuf index loading and conversion into the runtime LoadedIndex contract
purpose.internal/scipmodel: SCIP package and symbol identity parsing for stable grouping and display
purpose.internal/traversal: Read-only projection over SCIP documents, symbols, occurrences, metadata, and relationships
purpose.internal/traversal/traversaltest: Reusable SCIP fixture loading and accessors for traversal and query tests
purpose.internal/version: Build-time version metadata and fallback version reporting
purpose.root: Repository-level module metadata and documentation for the scip-search CLI
purpose.scripts: Distribution and release validation helper scripts

hints.add_feature: Start in cmd/scip-search/main.go to add or route a command, then keep query behavior in internal/query/* and expose only traversal.View-based read operations.

architecture.ai_summary:
scip-search is a Go CLI organized around a thin command entrypoint, a runtime/CLI layer, and focused query packages. cmd/scip-search wires subcommands to handlers; internal/cli handles argument routing, index loading, build identity, and status-oriented execution; internal/runtime standardizes loaded-index values, JSON success output, raw text output, diagnostics, and usage/index-load failures.

Data flows from a SCIP index file through internal/scipindex into runtime.LoadedIndex, then into internal/traversal.NewView, which exposes a read-only projection of documents, symbols, occurrences, metadata, and relationships. Query modules consume traversal.View rather than raw SCIP protobufs: discovery finds packages and symbols, references resolves occurrences for a symbol, implementations follows relationship edges, and graph composes definitions, references, contained dependencies, relationships, and test hints into static graph/impact views. Formatting is kept near each query, with shared one-line location helpers in internal/query/oneline and Markdown/JSON output where graph and impact commands need richer agent-facing summaries.

The project favors small internal packages with explicit contracts and golden-style tests around CLI/query output. internal/scipmodel centralizes SCIP identity parsing so discovery can group external symbols consistently, while internal/traversal/traversaltest provides reusable fixtures for behavior tests. Root shell scripts are isolated from the Go dependency graph and support installation/distribution workflows.
